skill.name: youtube-comments-api-skill
skill.description: Extract YouTube video metadata and public comments through the BrowserAct API using keywords, comment limits, and scroll counts.
skill.summary: Extract YouTube video results and public comments with BrowserAct automation.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static findings for API key access and network requests are true positives, but they match the stated BrowserAct API workflow. The markdown backtick and weak-crypto findings are false positives from documentation text, while the remaining concern is that user queries and an API key are sent to an external automation service.

medium_findings.title: External API Receives User Queries and Authorization Token
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The script sends user-provided YouTube search keywords to the BrowserAct workflow API with a bearer token. This is intended behavior, but it exposes user queries and API credentials to a third-party service and should be disclosed to users.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly constructs an Authorization header from the API key and posts a payload containing keywords to a BrowserAct endpoint. The behavior is clear and matches the skill purpose, so the risk is privacy and credential handling rather than confirmed malicious activity.

medium_findings.title: Long-Running Polling Against External Service
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The script polls BrowserAct task status and retrieves task output over the network. This is operationally expected, but users should understand that data retrieval depends on the external API and may expose returned public comment data in terminal output.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network polling and result retrieval calls are explicit and repeated until completion or timeout. There is no evidence of hidden destinations, but external dependency and data exposure remain real considerations.

low_findings.title: Expected Environment Variable Access for API Key
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE WITH LOW RESIDUAL RISK: The skill requires BROWSERACT_API_KEY and reads it from the environment. This is a standard credential pattern for API tools, and no evidence shows the key being printed or sent anywhere except the BrowserAct Authorization header.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable name and missing-key handling are explicit. Review found no evidence that the secret is logged, persisted, or transmitted to an unrelated host.

low_findings.title: Documentation Backticks Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The static analyzer flagged many markdown backticks and examples as Ruby or shell execution. The only actionable command is the documented Python invocation for the included script, not hidden dynamic command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are markdown documentation and field names, not executable code paths. The skill does instruct agents to run a local Python script, which is already captured as a scripts risk factor.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Review found no cryptographic code, hash verification logic, or weak algorithm use in the cited skill files. The high weak-cryptography findings appear to be pattern matches against ordinary documentation text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines describe the skill, input parameters, and output behavior. No evidence found for MD5, SHA-1, insecure random generation, or encryption operations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Scripted Network Automation with Credential Access
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines a local Python script, outbound HTTP requests, and an environment-provided API key. This combination warrants a marketplace warning, although the destination and purpose are disclosed in the skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly combines requests-based network calls with bearer-token authentication. The reviewed implementation uses a disclosed BrowserAct endpoint, so this is a risk pattern rather than confirmed abuse.

content.value_statement: YouTube comment research can be slow when teams need video metadata and audience reactions together. This skill runs a BrowserAct workflow that returns structured public video results and comment data from keyword searches.
actual_capabilities: Runs the included Python script with keywords, comment limits, and scroll counts.
Reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from the environment before calling BrowserAct.
Starts a BrowserAct YouTube comments workflow from a fixed template ID.
Polls task status until the workflow finishes, fails, or times out.
Prints returned video metadata and public comment fields from the API response.
Guides users to configure a BrowserAct API key when credentials are missing.
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key and network access to BrowserAct endpoints.
Depends on BrowserAct workflow availability and YouTube page behavior.
Does not perform sentiment analysis by itself; it returns source data for later analysis.
May print large comment results directly to the terminal without local redaction.

use_cases.title: Audience Research for Product Teams
use_cases.description: Collect comments around product keywords to identify repeated pain points, feature requests, and viewer objections.

use_cases.title: Content Topic Validation
use_cases.description: Review public reactions to YouTube search results before choosing topics, titles, or campaign angles.

use_cases.title: Competitive Comment Monitoring
use_cases.description: Gather video metadata and comments for competitor-related searches to compare audience concerns and engagement signals.

prompt_templates.title: Collect Basic Comments
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to search YouTube for "AI productivity" and collect up to 10 comments per video with 2 scrolls.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Two Topics
prompt_templates.prompt: Run separate searches for "AI coding assistant" and "AI code review". Summarize differences in viewer concerns after extraction.

prompt_templates.title: Find Product Feedback Themes
prompt_templates.prompt: Collect comments for "workflow automation tools" with 20 comments per video. Group repeated feature requests and objections.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Competitive Insight Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract comments for three competitor search terms, then produce a concise report with engagement signals, themes, and quoted public examples.

output_examples.input: Search for "AI productivity" with 10 comments per video.
output_examples.output: A structured summary of matching videos, including titles, URLs, publish timing, view counts, and selected public comments.

output_examples.input: Collect comments about "automation tools" and summarize feedback themes.
output_examples.output: A research brief showing common praise, concerns, feature requests, and representative public comment excerpts.

output_examples.input: Monitor competitor-related YouTube discussion for a product category.
output_examples.output: A comparison of viewer questions, objections, and engagement indicators across relevant public videos.

best_practices: Set BROWSERACT_API_KEY in the environment before running the script.
Start with small comment and scroll limits, then increase them when results are useful.
Review YouTube and BrowserAct terms before collecting or storing comment data.
anti_patterns: Do not paste API keys into prompts, chat messages, or shared transcripts.
Do not treat public comments as verified facts without independent review.
Do not request excessive collection when a smaller sample answers the research question.

faq.question: Does this skill require an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires BROWSERACT_API_KEY so the Python script can call the BrowserAct workflow API.

faq.question: Does it scrape YouTube directly from my browser?
faq.answer: No. The included script calls BrowserAct API endpoints that run the configured YouTube comments workflow.

faq.question: What data can it return?
faq.answer: It can return video titles, URLs, publish timing, view counts, comment authors, comment text, likes, and reply counts.

faq.question: Can it analyze sentiment automatically?
faq.answer: The skill collects comment data. Sentiment analysis must be performed in a later analysis step.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning. It uses expected network and credential access for a third-party API workflow.

faq.question: What should users avoid?
faq.answer: Users should avoid sharing API keys in prompts and should avoid collecting more public comment data than needed.
